_comment: WC2026 Group G, MD3. Standings before: Egypt 4 (1-1 Belgium, 3-1 NZ), Belgium 2, Iran 2 (2-2 NZ, 0-0 Belgium), NZ 1. Egypt near-top and the quality side (Salah); Iran is a PROVEN organized/defensive side (held Belgium 0-0) -> the organized-underdog archetype (England-Ghana / Colombia-DR Congo): Egypt favourite but the DRAW/Under is a live tail, don't overrate a rout. Iran needs a result too -> must-win edge. Home = Egypt, away = Iran. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Egypt (4 pts) tops the group with a draw; Iran (2 pts) needs a result to qualify. Egypt the quality side; Iran organized and hard to break down.

home.name: Egypt
notes: Salah-led; beat NZ 3-1, drew Belgium 1-1. attack 1.15.
Favourite but Iran's organization keeps the draw/Under live (held Belgium 0-0).

away.name: Iran
notes: Needs a result -> will compete; low-scoring read.

_lineups._comment: Confirmed/projected XIs (RotoWire/Sky, ~60 min pre-KO). Iran's shape is the key signal: a 5-4-1 ultra-defensive block with Taremi as the LONE striker -> pure containment, which REINFORCES the organized-underdog/Under read (cf. England-Ghana). Egypt field a full-strength attack (Salah + Marmoush both start). Net adjustment vs the strength baseline: trim Egypt 1.30 -> 1.25 (a packed 5-man box is harder to break) and Iran 0.92 -> 0.82 (a lone striker behind a bus generates little), tilting toward the draw/Under tail while keeping Egypt the favourite.
